modules.name: k8s
modules.description: Deploys an Oracle Database 23ai (Free) StatefulSet with a matching Service into a Kubernetes cluster, optionally fronted by a Traefik Ingress.

required_inputs.name: namespace
required_inputs.description: The namespace of the oracle-db StatefulSet and Service

optional_inputs.name: name
optional_inputs.description: The name of the oracle-db StatefulSet and Service

optional_inputs.name: replicas
optional_inputs.description: Number of Oracle 23ai StatefulSet replicas. Default `1` is fine for demos; the chart isn't HA-aware so larger values aren't useful without external coordination.

optional_inputs.name: storage_size
optional_inputs.description: PersistentVolumeClaim size requested per replica for `/opt/oracle/oradata`. Default `50Gi` covers a demo dataset; bump for benchmarks.

optional_inputs.name: image
optional_inputs.description: Container image for the Oracle 23ai database. Defaults to the public Oracle Free image; override to pin a tag or point at a mirrored registry.

optional_inputs.name: service_port
optional_inputs.description: Cluster-IP Service port that exposes the Oracle TNS listener. Defaults to the Oracle standard `1521`.

optional_inputs.name: container_port
optional_inputs.description: Pod port the TNS listener binds to inside the container. Defaults to `1521`; only change when overriding the image entrypoint.

optional_inputs.name: oracle_pwd
optional_inputs.description: SYS/SYSTEM/PDBADMIN password injected via the `ORACLE_PWD` env var. Demo default is intentionally low-effort — rotate when exposing the DB outside the cluster.

optional_inputs.name: oracle_characterset
optional_inputs.description: Database character set passed via the `ORACLE_CHARACTERSET` env var at first boot. AL32UTF8 is the Oracle-recommended UTF-8 default; only change for legacy compatibility.

optional_inputs.name: ingress
optional_inputs.description: Enable Ingress for the oracle-db service

optional_inputs.name: ingress_domain
optional_inputs.description: The domain for the ingress, default is `cloud`

optional_inputs.name: ingress_entrypoint
optional_inputs.description: The entrypoint to use for the ingress, default is `web`

optional_inputs.name: ingress_observability
optional_inputs.description: Emit Traefik observability signals (access logs, metrics, traces) for the Oracle 23ai ingress router. Set to false to add the three `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.observability.*: \"false\"` annotations. Same switch shape as other k8s modules.

optional_inputs.name: ingress_annotations
optional_inputs.description: Additional metadata annotations merged onto the Ingress. Useful for custom router options beyond the three observability toggles.

modules.name: k8s
modules.description: Bring-your-own Kubernetes cluster: adapt an existing cluster's kubeconfig to the same output shape this repo's managed-k8s modules expose (EKS, AKS, DOKS, ...). Downstream modules (observability, traefik, ai, security, tools) can then consume the imported cluster with no changes to their inputs.

required_inputs.name: kubeconfig
required_inputs.description: Full kubeconfig contents for the existing Kubernetes cluster. Pass via `file(\"~/.kube/config\")` or read from a `data.local_file`. The module extracts host / CA / token from the current context (or the first context if `current-context` is unset). Sensitive.

optional_inputs.name: cluster_name
optional_inputs.description: Logical name for the imported cluster — surfaced in outputs so downstream modules can tag resources consistently. Pure metadata; no resources are renamed based on it.

outputs.name: host
outputs.description: Imported cluster API server URL (parsed from kubeconfig).

outputs.name: cluster_ca_certificate
outputs.description: Imported cluster CA certificate (PEM, decoded from `certificate-authority-data`). Empty when the kubeconfig uses a CA file path or exec auth — pass the CA explicitly downstream in that case.

outputs.name: token
outputs.description: Imported cluster auth token (parsed from the kubeconfig's user). Empty when the kubeconfig uses exec auth — wire downstream providers via `config_path` to the kubeconfig file instead.

outputs.name: kubeconfig
outputs.description: Imported cluster kubeconfig, passed through unchanged. Use this when downstream providers can take `config_path` or a raw kubeconfig string.

outputs.name: cluster_id
outputs.description: Imported cluster identifier. Mirrors `cluster_name` since there is no provider-side cluster ID for an imported cluster.

outputs.name: cluster_name
outputs.description: Imported cluster name (echo of the input, for downstream tagging).
